# Nimbus Retention Controls PRD

Canonical URL: https://docs.google.com/document/d/GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd
Document ID: GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd
Revision: rev-2026-06-24-a
Status: Approved
Owner: Product Platform
Approved: 2026-06-24

## Purpose

Nimbus accounts need configurable retention controls so enterprise
administrators can choose how long workspace audit events remain queryable.
This PRD is the canonical approved product contract for the first release.

## Scope

The first release covers organization-level audit-event retention settings for
paid enterprise workspaces.

Included behavior:

- Administrators can choose 30, 90, 180, or 365 day audit-event retention.
- The default for newly created enterprise workspaces is 180 days.
- Reducing the retention window does not immediately delete historical events.
- Shortened retention applies on the next nightly retention enforcement job.
- Increasing the retention window preserves existing events that have not
  already expired.
- Workspace members without administrator permission cannot view or change the
  retention setting.
- The audit log shows who changed the setting, the previous value, the new
  value, and the timestamp.

Excluded behavior:

- Legal hold workflows.
- Per-user retention settings.
- Data export retention.
- Billing plan changes.
- Retention controls for non-enterprise workspaces.

## Scenarios

Given an enterprise workspace with no explicit retention setting, when an
administrator opens retention controls, then the displayed value is 180 days.

Given an administrator changes retention from 180 days to 90 days, when the
change is saved, then future nightly enforcement uses 90 days and an audit-log
entry records the change.

Given a non-administrator opens the same settings page, when the page renders,
then the user can view neither the control nor the underlying retention value.

Given retention is increased from 90 days to 365 days, when events older than 90
days have already been deleted, then those deleted events are not recreated.

Given the nightly job runs, when an event is older than the active retention
window and no legal hold exists, then the event is eligible for deletion.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Only enterprise administrators can read or change the retention setting.
- The allowed retention values are exactly 30, 90, 180, and 365 days.
- New enterprise workspaces default to 180 days.
- Every successful retention change creates an audit-log entry.
- Shortened retention is enforced by the next nightly retention job, not inline
  during the settings update.
- The implementation does not add legal hold behavior in this release.

## Rollout

Ship behind the existing enterprise settings feature flag. Enable for internal
enterprise workspaces first, then for ten design partners, then for all
enterprise workspaces after seven days without severity-one incidents.

## Open Questions

None. This PRD is approved for implementation as written.

Status: active
Created: 2026-06-28
Updated: 2026-06-28

# Nimbus Retention Controls

## Canonical Source

The approved Google Doc PRD is canonical for implementation planning and behavior:

- Canonical URL: https://docs.google.com/document/d/GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd
- Document ID: `GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd`
- Exported local artifact: `external-artifacts/google-docs/GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd.md`
- Revision: `rev-2026-06-24-a`
- PRD status: Approved
- Owner: Product Platform
- Approved: 2026-06-24

If this record conflicts with the Google Doc PRD revision above, the Google Doc PRD wins. Update this record only after confirming a new approved PRD revision.

## Purpose And Scope

Nimbus enterprise administrators need organization-level controls for how long workspace audit events remain queryable.

The first release includes only organization-level audit-event retention settings for paid enterprise workspaces.

## Behavior

- Enterprise administrators MUST be able to choose exactly one of these audit-event retention windows: 30, 90, 180, or 365 days.
- Newly created enterprise workspaces MUST default to 180 days when they have no explicit retention setting.
- Reducing the retention window MUST NOT delete historical events inline during the settings update.
- A shortened retention window MUST take effect on the next nightly retention enforcement job.
- Increasing the retention window MUST preserve existing events that have not already expired.
- Increasing the retention window MUST NOT recreate events that were already deleted.
- Workspace members without administrator permission MUST NOT be able to view or change the retention setting.
- Every successful retention setting change MUST create an audit-log entry that records:
  - actor who changed the setting
  - previous value
  - new value
  - timestamp
- When the nightly job runs, an event older than the active retention window and not under legal hold is eligible for deletion.

## Exclusions

The first release MUST NOT add:

- Legal hold workflows
- Per-user retention settings
- Data export retention controls
- Billing plan changes
- Retention controls for non-enterprise workspaces

## Rollout

Implementation MUST ship behind the existing enterprise settings feature flag.

Rollout order from the PRD:

1. Internal enterprise workspaces
2. Ten design partners
3. All enterprise workspaces after seven days without severity-one incidents

## Acceptance Criteria

- Only enterprise administrators can read or change the retention setting.
- Allowed retention values are exactly 30, 90, 180, and 365 days.
- New enterprise workspaces default to 180 days.
- Every successful retention change creates an audit-log entry.
- Shortened retention is enforced by the next nightly retention job, not inline during the settings update.
- Implementation does not add legal hold behavior in this release.

## Implementation Planning Notes

The implementation codebase is not present in this workspace. Future execution planning should first locate the Nimbus source repository and inspect existing enterprise settings, audit log, workspace plan/permission, feature-flag, and nightly retention-job patterns before opening executable child tickets.

Candidate implementation slices, subject to source inspection:

- Persistence and defaulting for the organization-level enterprise audit-event retention setting.
- Administrator-only read/change surface in enterprise settings.
- Settings update behavior that validates allowed values and emits audit-log entries.
- Nightly retention enforcement integration using the active retention window.
- Feature-flag and rollout configuration for the staged release.

## References

- Evidence: `.10x/evidence/2026-06-28-nimbus-retention-prd-export.md`
- Planning ticket: `.10x/tickets/2026-06-28-nimbus-retention-controls-planning.md`

# Nimbus Retention Controls Implementation Planning

## Scope

Plan implementation of the approved Nimbus Retention Controls PRD into bounded executable tickets after the Nimbus implementation codebase is available.

The canonical product contract is the approved Google Doc PRD indexed by `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-controls.md`.

## Current Blocker

The target Nimbus implementation repository/source tree is absent from this workspace. This workspace contains the exported Google Doc PRD only, so executable tickets would require guessing the persistence model, permission APIs, audit-log mechanism, feature-flag system, settings UI/API boundaries, and nightly retention job ownership.

## Acceptance Criteria For Unblocking

- The target Nimbus source repository is available for inspection.
- Existing enterprise settings feature flag behavior is identified.
- Existing workspace plan and administrator permission checks are identified.
- Existing audit-log event conventions are identified.
- Existing audit-event retention or nightly deletion job ownership is identified.
- Existing settings UI/API boundaries are identified, if applicable.

## Candidate Child Tickets After Source Inspection

These are planning candidates, not executable tickets yet:

- Add organization-level audit-event retention persistence and 180-day enterprise default.
- Add administrator-only read/change behavior for the retention setting.
- Validate retention updates against exactly 30, 90, 180, and 365 days.
- Emit audit-log entries for successful retention changes with actor, previous value, new value, and timestamp.
- Integrate active retention windows into the nightly retention enforcement job without inline deletion on settings update.
- Wire the release behind the existing enterprise settings feature flag and staged rollout controls.

## Explicit Exclusions

- No code implementation in this workspace.
- No legal hold workflow implementation.
- No per-user retention settings.
- No data export retention behavior.
- No billing plan changes.
- No non-enterprise retention controls.

## Evidence Expectations

When unblocked, child tickets should record evidence for:

- Permission tests proving non-administrators cannot read or change the value.
- Validation tests for the exact allowed values.
- Defaulting tests for new enterprise workspaces.
- Audit-log tests for successful changes.
- Retention job tests proving shortened windows are enforced by the nightly job and not inline.
- Regression coverage proving already-deleted events are not recreated when retention increases.
- Feature-flag or rollout evidence matching the approved rollout plan.

## References

- Spec/index: `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-controls.md`
- PRD export: `external-artifacts/google-docs/GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd.md`
- Evidence: `.10x/evidence/2026-06-28-nimbus-retention-prd-export.md`

## Progress And Notes

- 2026-06-28: Created planning owner from the approved Google Doc PRD export. Blocked implementation decomposition on access to the target Nimbus source repository.
